ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR FEB-24-2026 CONTINUED TO JUN-02-2026 AT 10:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 610 FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN JUDGMENT. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-15-26 09:00 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CONSUMER PORTFOLIO SERVICES, INC.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO ENTER JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO STIPULATION IS GRANTED. GOOD CAUSE APPEARING AND CONSISTENT WITH THE PARTIES' AGREEMENT AN D CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 664.6, JUDGMENT SHALL BE ENTERED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF CONSUMER PORTFOLIO SERVICES, INC. SHALL RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT SHAREE BRANNER $9,502.79 IN PRINCIPAL AND $315 IN COSTS, FOR A TOTAL JUDGMENT OF $9,817.79. THIS IS THE JUDGMENT AUTHORIZED BY THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND RELATED EVENTS. PRIOR TO THE TIME SET FOR HEARING, PLAINTIFF SHALL LODGE BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG A PROPOSED ORDER CONTAINING THE ABOVE TEXT VERBATIM. PRIOR TO THE TIME SET FOR HEARING, PLAINTIFF SHALL LODGE BY EMAIL TO CONTESTDEPT302TR@SFTC.ORG A PROPOSED JUDGMENT CONFORMING TO THE REQUIREMENTS IN THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE AND CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT, IDEALLY ON THE APPROPRIATE JUDICIAL COUNCIL FORM. NO PROPOSED ORDER NOR PROPOSED JUDGMENT RECEIVED. PLAINTIFF FAILED TO PROVIDE AND FOLLOW THE COURT'S ORDER. COUNSEL FOR THE PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE COURT'S RULING, A PROPOSED JUDGMENT AND MUST DROP A HARD COURTESY COPY IN DEPT. 302'S INBOX. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar May-29-2026 Continued To Aug-04-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation
Off Calendar Apr-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

Plaintiff Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc.'s unopposed Motion To Enter Judgment Pursuant To Stipulation is GRANTED.

Good cause appearing and consistent with the parties' agreement and Code of Civil Procedure section 664.6, judgment shall be entered as follows: Plaintiff Consumer Portfolio Services, Inc. shall recover from Defendant Sharee Branner $9,502.79 in principal and $315 in costs, for a total judgment of $9,817.79. This is the judgment authorized by the settlement agreement and related events.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed order containing the above text verbatim.

Prior to the time set for hearing, Plaintiff shall lodge by email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org a proposed judgment conforming to the requirements in the Code of Civil Procedure and California Rules of Court, ideally on the appropriate Judicial Council form.

No proposed order nor proposed judgment received. Plaintiff failed to provide and follow the court's order.

Counsel for the Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling, a proposed judgment and must drop a hard courtesy copy in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
